LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF GRANT SAXENA'S MOTION FOR ORDER TO DISQUALIFY COUNSEL. PRO PER PLAINTIFF'S LARGELY INCOMPREHENSIBLE MOTION TO DISQUALIFY COUNSEL IS DENIED, WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO FILING AN UNDERSTANDABLE MOTION. THE CURRENT MOTION'S LEAD ARGUMENT IS THAT TWO DEFENDANTS "ARE NOT ENTITLED TO ANY REPRESENTATION IN THIS CIVIL ACTION," BUT WHY IS NOT ARTICULATED. (MOT. 3:11-12.) NOR ARE THE MOTION'S UNCLEAR ALLEGATIONS AS TO "PRIVILEGED AND CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" SUPPORTED BY ANY COMPETENT EVIDENCE. (ID. AT 5:1-15.) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/RBU)

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Oct-07-2024 Dismissal - No Appearance

MOTION TO CONTINUE COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Oct-02-2024 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Disqualify Counsel
Off Calendar Sep-11-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Taghi Astanehe, Esq., The Law Office of Taghi Astanehe, 650-281-6342 Taghi4193@yahoo.com.
Pro Per Defendant
No other appearances.

Plaintiff contested the Court's tenative ruling by email on 9/10/24. No appearance was made by Plaintiff on the day of the hearing on 9/11/24.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF GRANT SAXENA's Motion For Order To Disqualify Counsel.

Pro per plaintiff's largely incomprehensible motion to disqualify counsel is denied, without prejudice to filing an understandable motion. The current motion's lead argument is that two defendants "are not entitled to any representation in this civil action," but why is not articulated. (Mot. 3:11-12.) Nor are the motion's unclear allegations as to "privileged and confidential information" supported by any competent evidence. (Id. at 5:1-15.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Strike Answers
Off Calendar Sep-04-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF GRANT SAXENA's Motion For Order To Strike Answers.

Off calendar. Motion was not filed and served in compliance with CCP 1005.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
